since_last: 33% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +1.92%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 33% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX -0.17% · BTC -2.85% · Gold +1.51% · Nasdaq100 +0.40%. 33% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +1.92%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +1.92%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -3.15%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +14.70%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +14.70%, AMAT +11.67%, TER +8.47%, KLAC +7.09%, LRCX +5.75%. Laggards: INOD -12.00%, DELL -7.19%, OKLO -6.14%, OKLO -6.14%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.43% at $152.32
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.43% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
